title: Jones v. Alfred H. Mayer Co.
summary: The Supreme Court held that Congress could prohibit private racial discrimination in the sale or rental of property under 42 U.S.C. § 1982 and the Thirteenth Amendment.
outcome_summary: The ruling strengthened federal power against private housing discrimination beyond state action cases alone.
impact_notes: An important housing-rights decision expanding protection against private racial discrimination.

scores.notes: Important reinforcement of federal anti-discrimination power in housing.

sources.source_title: Jones v. Alfred H. Mayer Co., 392 U.S. 409 (1968)

title: Alexander v. Holmes County Board of Education
summary: The Supreme Court ordered Southern school districts to end delays and transition immediately from dual school systems to unitary systems.
outcome_summary: The ruling accelerated school desegregation after years of slow-walking and resistance across the South.
impact_notes: Forced more immediate implementation of desegregation in K-12 education.

scores.notes: Important desegregation ruling aimed at ending deliberate delay.

sources.source_title: Alexander v. Holmes County Board of Education, 396 U.S. 19 (1969)

title: Swann v. Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education
summary: The Supreme Court approved broad equitable remedies, including busing and redrawn attendance zones, to dismantle de jure school segregation.
outcome_summary: The ruling gave federal courts stronger tools to implement desegregation in public schools where anti-Black segregation had been maintained by law and policy.
impact_notes: A major pro-desegregation decision in K-12 education.

scores.notes: Strong remedy case for school desegregation.

sources.source_title: Swann v. Charlotte-Mecklenburg Board of Education, 402 U.S. 1 (1971)
